sa:features.rdfs:label: Description
sa:features.sa:description: Draegloths are hulking, four-armed horrors born of dark pacts between drow priestesses and demonic entities. Standing over eight feet tall with claws like sabers and a constant aura of menace, they are bred for violence and domination. Though often mistaken for mindless beasts, Draegloths possess a cruel cunning and an instinctive understanding of power dynamics. They serve dark cults or carve their own brutal empires among the void-born and forgotten.

sa:features.rdfs:label: Claws
sa:features.sa:description: Unarmed attacks deal 1d6 slashing damage. As a bonus action after taking the attack action, you can attack once using your unarmed strike.

sa:relationFeatures.rdfs:label: Human relations
sa:relationFeatures.sa:description: Tolerated only as pawns or sacrifices. Their unpredictability is amusing, and short lives cement them as disposable.

sa:relationFeatures.rdfs:label: Elf relations
sa:relationFeatures.sa:description: Detested for their arrogance and ancient judgments. Killing an elf is seen as a sacred act of defiance.

sa:relationFeatures.rdfs:label: Drow relations
sa:relationFeatures.sa:description: Considered 'mother-kin'—the only beings shown respect, and even then, only if they demonstrate cruelty and dominance. A weak drow is a disgrace.

sa:relationFeatures.rdfs:label: Dwarf relations
sa:relationFeatures.sa:description: Hunted for sport. Their resilience and armor make them worthy prey. Draegloths take great pleasure in breaking dwarven lines.

sa:relationFeatures.rdfs:label: Halfling relations
sa:relationFeatures.sa:description: Appetizers. Their terror is intoxicating. Some Draegloths believe halfling screams are musically blessed.

sa:relationFeatures.rdfs:label: Orc relations
sa:relationFeatures.sa:description: Sometimes spared—briefly—for strength duels or gladiatorial rituals. Orc ferocity is admired, but only as a challenge to overcome.
